CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-04-2015 Continued To Apr-29-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO TRANSFER
Off Calendar Feb-27-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. ANDREW P. MCDEVITT of Law Offices of Walkup, Melodia, Kelly & Schoenberger APC, Tel No. (415) 981-7210 appearing for the Plaintiffs;
Attys. JOHN E. FAGAN (Tel No. 530-550-2055) and MICHAEL L. REITZELL (Tel No. 415-957-3084) of Duane Morris LLP appearing for Defendant KSL Capital Partners LLC, Squaw Valley Restory LLC, Squaw Valley Ski Holdings LLC, and JMA Ventures LLC.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS KSL CAPITAL PARTNERS LLC, SQUAW VALLEY RESORT LLC, SQUAW VALLEY SKI HOLDINGS LLC, AND JMA VENTURES LLC'S MOTION TO TRANSFER is denied. Case law establishes two factors that the court must consider in determining whether a defendant is joined solely for venue purposes - (1) whether the plaintiff added the defendant in good faith and (2) whether it would be possible for a cause of action to be pled against the defendant. (Gutierrez v. Sup. Ct. (1966) 243 Cal.App.2d 710, 725.) The Court finds that Plaintiffs' evidence establishes that Defendant JMA Ventures, LLC ("JMA") was added as a defendant in good faith. The Declaration of Andrew McDevitt shows that Plaintiffs had a reasonable basis for alleging that JMA is involved in the ownership or operation of Squaw Valley Resort. (McDevitt Decl., Exs. 3, 5.) If the plaintiff has "reasonable grounds for the belief in good faith that plaintiff had a cause of action against the resident defendant'then we do not believe that it may be said that the resident defendant was improperly joined as a defendant or was made a defendant solely for the purpose of having the action tried in the county of the residence of the resident defendant within the meaning of section 395 of the Code of Civil Procedure." (Peiser v. Mettler (1958) 50 Cal.2d 594, 603.) Some cases also require a showing that the plaintiff can conceivably state a cause of action against the defendant in order to survive a motion to transfer venue. However, it is a low standard and the "trial court does not need to try, upon conflicting affidavits, the issues of facts going to the merits of the cause of action stated against the resident defendant." (California Collection Agency v. Fontana (1943) 61 Cal.App.2d 648, 653.) "Allegations which merely shadow forth the semblance of a cause of action against such defendant are proof against assault on the ground that they are sham and frivolous, provided such cause of action is apparently pleaded in good faith and is not prima facie so glaringly and vitally defective as to be beyond correction by amendment." (Gutierrez v. Sup. Ct. (1966) 243 Cal.App.2d 710, 726, internal quotations omitted.) The allegations against JMA are not so "glaringly and vitally defective" that the Court can declare JMA a sham defendant. PLAINTIFFS COUNSEL SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Paula J. Shi, CSR #13358 (302/EHG)

MOTION TO TRANSFER
Off Calendar Feb-03-2015 Continued To Feb-27-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS KSL CAPITAL PARTNERS LLC, SQUAW VALLEY RESORT LLC, SQUAW VALLEY SKI HOLDINGS LLC, AND JMA VENTURES LLC's MOTION TO TRANSFER is continued on the Court's motion to February 27, 2015. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/EHG)
